note: Section Library saves Layout Builder sections as reusable CONTENT: each saved section/page is a `section_library_template` content entity (base table `section_library_template`) with a `label`, a `type` of `section` or `template`, and the serialized layout in an unlimited-cardinality `layout_section` field. There is no plugin type and no Drush; the only config object is `section_library.settings` (two relabel keys). Easy `recipe` cases (response-graded) probe the entity/data model, the save/reuse flow, and the permission machine names. Medium `introspection` cases install two known templates and ask the agent to read the live library. Hard `execution` cases have the agent create a specific template entity, verified against live state.

evals.prompt: In the Section Library module on Drupal 11, when an editor saves a Layout Builder section to the library, what kind of thing is stored (config or content?), what is its entity type id, and where are saved templates managed in the admin?
evals.expected_output: Each saved section/page is stored as a `section_library_template` CONTENT entity (not config; base table `section_library_template`), managed at Admin -> Content -> Section library (`/admin/content/section-library`).

evals.prompt: Section Library can save either a single section or a whole page of sections. In the section_library_template entity, which field distinguishes the two and what are its two allowed values? Which field holds the actual serialized Layout Builder layout?
evals.expected_output: The `type` field (a list_string) with allowed values `section` (a single section) and `template` (a whole page). The serialized Layout Builder section(s) live in the unlimited-cardinality `layout_section` field.

evals.prompt: List the machine names of the Section Library permissions, in particular the one required to import a saved template into a layout and the one that grants full administration of the template entities.
evals.expected_output: Six permissions: `import template from section library` (import into a layout), `view section library templates`, `add section library templates`, `edit section library templates`, `delete section library templates`, and `administer section library template entities` (full admin).

evals.prompt: This Drupal site already has some Section Library templates saved. Inspect the live site (drush is available) and tell me the label of every saved section_library_template entity, and for each whether it is a single 'section' or a whole-page 'template'.
evals.expected_output: Two saved templates: 'Eval Landing Page' (type=template, a whole page) and 'Eval Promo Section' (type=section, a single section).

evals.prompt: On this site, look at the saved Section Library template labelled 'Eval Landing Page'. How many Layout Builder sections does its layout_section field hold, and what is the layout id of its first section?
evals.expected_output: 'Eval Landing Page' holds 2 sections in its layout_section field; the first section's layout id is `layout_onecol` (the second is `layout_twocol_section`).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, create a Section Library entry so editors can reuse it. Add a `section_library_template` content entity labelled exactly 'Homepage Hero' that is a whole-page template (type `template`) and contains a single one-column Layout Builder section (layout id `layout_onecol`). Save it to the live site so it appears in the section library.
evals.expected_output: Create a `section_library_template` entity with label 'Homepage Hero', type 'template', and one `layout_section` value that is a Layout Builder Section with layout id `layout_onecol` (e.g. via drush php:eval using \Drupal\layout_builder\Section and the entity storage), then save it.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, add a reusable single section to the Section Library: create a `section_library_template` content entity labelled exactly 'Promo Banner' with type `section` (a single section, not a whole page) whose layout is a two-column Layout Builder section (layout id `layout_twocol_section`). Persist it to the running site.
evals.expected_output: Create a `section_library_template` entity with label 'Promo Banner', type 'section', and one `layout_section` value that is a Layout Builder Section with layout id `layout_twocol_section`, then save it.
